FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study, baseline N=11,878, Years 1-4 longitudinal data collection beginning 2015 with 6-month intervals for 10 years.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: multi-level longitudinal models examining smartphone ownership impact on parent-child informant discrepancy of youth internalizing problems.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in Development and Psychopathology 2025.

FT_IC2.evidence: Smartphone ownership (yes/no, age of first ownership) is IV. DV is parent-child discrepancy in reporting internalizing problems (mental health outcome). Pairs SM with individual-trait-level variable (internalizing problems).

FT_IC3.evidence: Inferential longitudinal analyses reported: regression coefficients with significance (b = -0.21, SE = .042, p < .001, 95% CI [-0.29, -0.23]); multi-group models with parameter estimates; Time × Informant effects reported in Table 3.

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results throughout: Figure 1 shows slopes (β = .29***, β = -.24*); Table 3 parameter estimates with p-values for direct effects of time and informant on internalizing problems by age group.

FT_EC7_reason: Design is longitudinal, but smartphone is IV (ownership predicts later discrepancy), not DV-only. EC7 does not apply.
notes: All cascaded criteria met. Paper included.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD study, T1 N=11,787 (representative U.S. adolescent sample); baseline follow-up data collection.

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: longitudinal bidirectional associations between electronic media use and family conflict across 3 years.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in Journal of Family Psychology 2025.

FT_IC2.evidence: Electronic media use and social electronic media use are IV. Family conflict is DV (behavioral/family-relationship individual-trait-level outcome). Methods specify separate models for total EMU, video-viewing EMU, and social EMU.

FT_IC3.evidence: Inferential models examining associations: bidirectional panel models tested; cross-lagged associations examined. Main result: more overall electronic media use at T1 related to increases in family conflict at T2 and T3.

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results reported: Model fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA); regression coefficients (β = 0.10, p = .001, p-adj < .001 for total EMU→family conflict); path coefficients in cross-lagged models; Figure 1 shows standardized coefficients on paths.

FT_EC7_reason: Longitudinal design with bidirectional associations; SM/EM tested as both IV and DV (cross-lagged). EC7 requires DV-only with no IV analysis; this paper includes SM-as-IV analyses. EC7 does not apply.
notes: All cascaded criteria met. Paper included.

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study (5.1 release), Baseline N ~12,000, Mage=9.9, White=52.0%, Hispanic=20.3%, Black=15.0%, Asian=2.1%, Other=10.5%, Female=47.8%. Four-year follow-up (Y0, Y2, Y3, Y4).

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: parallel process latent growth curve models examining whether growth trajectories in social media use and cognitive performance (crystallized abilities, inhibitory control/attention, processing speed) were associated over 4 years.

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed article published in Journal of Research on Adolescence 2025.

FT_IC2.evidence: Social media use (time on social media, self-reported hours) is IV. Outcomes are cognitive abilities (crystallized abilities, inhibitory control/attention, processing speed) — all individual-trait-level outcomes within cognitive domain. Methods measure social media via survey questions about weekday/weekend hours.

FT_IC3.evidence: Parallel process latent growth curve models test associations between SM and cognitive trajectories: cross-construct intercept-slope covariances estimated and reported.

FT_IC4.evidence: Multiple quantitative results reported in Table 3: intercept-slope covariances (i_cryst ↔ s_sm: β = −.04, p = .024; s_cryst ↔ s_sm: β = −.36, p < .001; i_flanker ↔ i_sm: β = .06, p = .001; s_pattern ↔ s_sm: β = .11, p = .001). Parallel process models show negative coupling between SM trajectory and crystallized/inhibitory control trajectories.

FT_EC7_reason: Longitudinal design with SM as IV (predictor of cognitive trajectory change), not DV. EC7 requires SM-as-DV-only; this paper uses SM as IV. EC7 does not apply.
notes: All cascaded criteria met. Paper included.
